results.title: How to Sideload (install 3rd party APP) for Dilink5? : r/BYD - Reddit
results.snippet: Start off like the comment above but name the folder “Third Party Apps” afterwards make sure to format the drive to FAT32, install all your APKs.
results.summary:
### How to Sideload (install 3rd party APP) for Dilink5?
To sideload a third-party app on the Dilink5, follow these steps:
1. **Enable Unknown Sources**: Go to the device's settings, navigate to the security section, and allow installations from unknown sources.
This permission is necessary for installing apps outside of the official app store.
2. **Download the APK File**: Find a reliable source to download the APK file of the app you want to install.
Ensure that the source is trustworthy to avoid any security risks.
3. **Transfer the APK to Dilink5**: If you downloaded the APK on another device, transfer it to the Dilink5 using a USB drive or by connecting both devices over a network.
4. **Install the APK**: Locate the APK file using a file manager on your Dilink5.
Click on the file to start the installation process.
Follow the on-screen prompts to complete the installation.
5. **Launch the App**: Once installed, you can find the app in your app drawer.
Open it and enjoy the new features it offers.
Make sure to regularly check for updates for the app to ensure optimal performance and security.
**votes: 3**
**comments: 6**

Start off like the comment above but name the folder “Third Party Apps” afterwards make sure to format the drive to FAT32, install all your APKs, I would recommend GBox because YouTube by itself won’t work.
Make sure the car is off then insert the drive then turn on the car, you should get a screen that asks for what apps you wish to install then a password request, I do not know the password as it varies.
It should start downloading after the password is typed

Place the APK files within that folder.
The password for access is BYD6215F.

results.title: How to run Android Auto without Google apps - e/OS community
results.snippet: Activate USB debugging on your phone's developer settings. Connect to your phone in terminal with adb devices and install all these three apps with a command.
results.summary:
div
I managed to make Android Auto work without the Google apps.
All credits of this goes to **dylanderdaly** and **sn-00-x** in Github who are behind these stubs.
Instead of Google Maps, Google App and Google TTS I decided to try these stubs before installing the Android Auto app.
Download these to your computer:
Google App stub
Google Maps stub if it doesn’t start to download, click on **View raw**
Google TTS stub if it doesn’t start to download, click on **View raw**
Activate USB debugging on your phone’s developer settings.
Connect to your phone in terminal with
`adb devices`
and install all these three apps with a command:
`adb install name_of_the_app.apk`
After this install Android Auto normally as guided in the official instructions.
You might even succeed by uninstalling Google apps and installing stubs with adb.
**Regain your privacy!
Adopt /e/OS the deGoogled mobile OS and online services**
great, thanks a lot.
This also works for me without root, at least partly.
It didn’t work right away though.
I had to go to the android settings and search for android auto and then open the Android auto settings.
Only then a connection was automatically established between the phone and the car radio.
Also the apps didn’t want to work right away.
I had activated the Android auto development settings and checked “unknown sources”
Then I installed the VLC app from aurora store.
I had to disconnect the usb-cable and reconnect.
Then the app appeared on the app launcher on the car radio and it worked.
Not sure if the development settings + unknown resources is required, but I’ve read it is in order to get some apps into the launcher.
…
We are on the same boat when it comes to navigation apps.
...
Magic Earth behaves the same way for me, only **no new messages is** shown.
The funny part of this is that when I connect my phone to my computer and run AA emulator in Android Studio, all the 3rd party apps are visible.
...
I even tried the Headunit Reloaded trial and it also shows in self service mode all the 3rd party apps but again when I connect the phone to my car, only few apps are visible.
...
Maybe it works installing the APK’s with the King Installer it seems to work also without root
…
it would be nice to have this feature build into /e/OS.
...
It may work but for me it makes no sense - first we install degoogled /e/OS then we install Google app and Gmaps It would be nice to have some working verstion of AndroidAuto built into the system and be able to use it with some other map app than Gmaps… I think nowadays car connectivity is a standard and absolute minimum for phone OS… I have some other applications problems but I can live without them, but AA - if I won’t solve it in few month I will consider switching back to Android…
if AA is absolutely essential to you, you could check out GrapheneOS and AA.
I will just keep using my smartphone for navigation, if AA and /e/OS doesn’t work yet.
Graphene’s compatibility list covers just Pixel devices, so it cannot be used…
You can get magic earth work on android auto if you uninstall preinstalled via pm tool.
After that install the offical magic earth via app lounge.
Its just compatible with the officall google play store version/apk.

results.title: BYD DiLink | BYD Nigeria
results.snippet: BYD DiLink platform is equipped with Android intelligent system, which ... The screen can be rotated by voice control to meet with users' demands.
results.summary:
(Depending on the model and features of your vehicle, the availability of the services may vary.)
BYD DiLink platform is equipped with Android intelligent system, which brings smarter,safer and more comfortable driving experience to vehicle owners.
## More comfortable driving
- Voice Recognition based human-computer interaction allows users to control the car more conveniently.
- Providing multiple and customized sound effects for users.
- Navigation apps designed for vehicles, making it easier for living trips.
- Interconnection by WI-FI, BT and LTE makes smarter travels.
## Smarter entertainment
- It is the world’s first rotatable PAD.
The screen can be rotated by voice control to meet with users’ demands.
-
- - Split screen allows to put different apps in the right-side and left-side screens, to meet users needs on driving and entertainment simultaneously.
-
- - Users can choose different UI modes.
...
- In-vehicle devices can be controlled by touching screen and voice recognition, realizing a starter control and more effective interaction.
- Cloud service facilitates vehicle management by remote control.
- Lifelong free OTA enables the vehicle to realize consistent improvements.
## Safer driving
- The customized smart vehicle control system enables users to control in-vehicle devices and check vehicle status by touching screen and voice recognition, providing more comfortable driving experiences for users.
-
-
Users can choose from light mode, dark mode, and auto mode by their own.

results.title: Possible Way to Add CarPlay to Chinese Version of BYD DiLink 4.0 ...
results.snippet: It has Android Auto but no Carplay (wired nor wireless). I'm able to sideload apps and have updated the firmware to a version 202309 (from ...
results.summary:
From what I’ve gathered, the head unit hardware in this model runs on the Qualcomm QCM6125 PCB, which should theoretically be capable of supporting CarPlay, Android Auto, and other features with the right software.
It seems possible that building a custom ROM specifically for the Qualcomm QCM6125 could add:
• Apple CarPlay and Android Auto support
• Multi-language support for the instrument cluster (IVI) and infotainment interface
• Wireless casting and other Android-based connectivity features
• Potentially more internationalization options and additional app compatibility
I’m wondering if anyone here has experience with developing custom ROMs for the DiLink system or for similar Android-based head units using the Qualcomm QCM6125 platform.
